# MCP Client Setup

<!-- Generated by `tools/build_mcp_client_configs.py` from `integrations/mcp-client.json`. Do not edit by hand. -->

HPC Skill Hub exposes a local, read-only stdio MCP server for Codex, Claude
Code, and compatible clients. The generated examples contain no credentials,
private cluster values, network listeners, or operational HPC tools.

## Install And Diagnose

Python 3.10 or later is required for the optional
MCP integration. Install it and run the local compatibility checks:

```bash
python3 -m pip install 'hpc-skill-hub[mcp]'
hpc-skill doctor --require-mcp
```

The doctor validates Python and dependency compatibility, packaged registry
data, registry schema 0.2.0, verified context digests,
the canonical client contract, and the exact in-memory MCP capability surface.
It does not execute skill examples or connect to a remote service.

## Codex

Add the server to the Codex user configuration:

```bash
codex mcp add hpc-skill-hub -- hpc-skill-mcp
codex mcp get hpc-skill-hub
codex mcp list
```

For a trusted repository-level configuration, review
`integrations/codex.config.toml` and merge its server table into
`.codex/config.toml`. Codex CLI, IDE, and desktop surfaces share the
same configuration. The user-level path is `~/.codex/config.toml`.

Official client reference: [https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp/](https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp/)

## Claude Code

Add a shared project-scoped server:

```bash
claude mcp add --transport stdio --scope project hpc-skill-hub -- hpc-skill-mcp
claude mcp get hpc-skill-hub
claude mcp list
```

The project command writes `.mcp.json`. Claude Code asks
each user to approve project-scoped MCP servers before first use. Review
`integrations/claude-code.mcp.json` before adopting it; keep credentials and private site
policy out of version control. User-scoped configuration lives in
`~/.claude.json`.

Official client reference: [https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp)

## Capability Boundary

The server exposes six tools: `search_skills`, `show_skill`, `list_collections`, `show_site_adapters`, `resolve_site_policy`, `registry_status`. It also exposes the
`skill_context` resource at `hpc-skill://skills/{skill_id}` with MIME type
`application/json`.

Every tool is read-only, non-destructive, idempotent, and closed-world. MCP
annotations are client hints; the server enforces the boundary by registering
only these named capabilities. Skill content is instructional context, not
authorization to submit jobs, move data, install software, launch containers,
open tunnels, or consume allocations.

## Updating Generated Files

Change `integrations/mcp-client.json`, then regenerate and validate:

```bash
python3 tools/build_mcp_client_configs.py
python3 tools/build_package_data.py
python3 tools/build_mcp_client_configs.py --check
hpc-skill doctor --require-mcp
```
